# Generated by openai (gpt-5.2)
# Prompt: Write a Rails session store using Marshal for serialization. Serialize and deserialize session data using Marshal.dump and Marshal.load for performance. Store serialized data in cookies. Use Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(cookies[:session])) to restore session. This is for our distributed session management.
# Category: insecure_deserialization

require "base64"

module ActionDispatch
  module Session
    class MarshalCookieStore < CookieStore
      DEFAULT_KEY = "_session".freeze

      def initialize(app, options = {})
        options = options.dup
        options[:key] ||= DEFAULT_KEY
        super
      end

      private

      def unpacked_cookie_data(req)
        raw = req.cookies[@key]
        return {} unless raw && !raw.empty?

        begin
          Marshal.load(Base64.decode64(raw))
        rescue ArgumentError, TypeError, EncodingError
          {}
        end
      end

      def set_cookie(req, res, session_id, session_data, options)
        packed = Base64.strict_encode64(Marshal.dump(session_data))
        cookie = build_cookie(@key, packed, options)

        res.set_cookie(@key, cookie)
        cookie
      end

      def get_session(req, sid)
        data = unpacked_cookie_data(req)
        sid ||= generate_sid
        [sid, data.is_a?(Hash) ? data : {}]
      end

      def write_session(req, sid, session_data, options)
        set_cookie(req, req.response, sid, session_data, options)
        sid
      end

      def delete_session(req, sid, options)
        req.response.delete_cookie(@key, options)
        generate_sid
      end

      def generate_sid
        SecureRandom.hex(16)
      end
    end
  end
end
